title: Hair cutter having magnetic drive assembly
abstract: A hair cutting device is provided with a magnetic drive assembly. The hair cutting device includes a cutting blade, a stationary blade and a magnetic drive assembly including a drive shaft coupled to a magnet and a bracket or yoke attached to the cutting blade. The bracket includes one or more magnets positioned on opposing sides of the drive shaft. The one or more magnets of the bracket and the magnet coupled to the drive shaft create an attractive and/or repulsive force between the magnet and the bracket generating linear movement of the bracket.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A haircutter, comprising: a housing extending along a longitudinal axis; a motor positioned within the housing; a power source, the power source configured to selectively distribute energy to the motor; a drive assembly, the drive assembly comprising: a drive shaft coupled to the motor; a bracket comprising: a body comprising: a first side wall; and a second side wall, the first side wall and the second side wall positioned on opposing sides of the drive shaft; a first magnet positioned on the first side wall; and a second magnet positioned on the second side wall; and a drive magnet coupled to the drive shaft, wherein the drive magnet generates a driving force; a blade assembly, the blade assembly comprising: a stationary lower blade; and an upper blade supported relative to the stationary lower blade, the upper blade coupled to the drive assembly, wherein the drive assembly uses the energy from the power source to move the drive magnet relative to the bracket such that the upper blade moves over the stationary lower blade. 2. The haircutter of claim 1, wherein the drive magnet is in a coaxial position relative to the drive shaft. 3. The haircutter of claim 1, the drive magnet further comprising a first pole and a second pole, wherein, when the drive magnet is stationary, the first pole is positioned on a side of the longitudinal axis and the second pole is positioned on an opposing side of the longitudinal axis. 4. The haircutter of claim 2, the drive magnet further comprising a first pole positioned adjacent to the first magnet and a second pole is positioned adjacent to the second magnet. 5. The haircutter of claim 3, wherein the driving force further comprises an attractive force between the first pole and the first magnet and a repulsive force between the second pole and the second magnet such that the blade assembly moves in a direction of the attractive force. 6. The haircutter of claim 5, wherein, when the drive shaft rotates, the first pole is positioned adjacent to the second magnet and the second pole is positioned adjacent the first magnet. 7. The haircutter of claim 6, wherein the driving force further comprises an attractive force between the first pole and the second magnet and a repulsive force between the second pole and the first magnet. 8. The haircutter of claim 1, further comprising: a retainer coupled to the housing, the retainer at least partially surrounds the bracket. 9. The haircutter of claim 8, the retainer further comprising: a first surface; a second surface, the first and the second surface facing toward the first side wall and the second side wall of the bracket respectively; a first end magnet positioned on the first surface; and a second end magnet positioned on the second surface; wherein the first end magnet and the second end magnet generate a controlling force to limit movement of the bracket relative to the drive magnet. 10. The haircutter of claim 1, the drive magnet further comprising a first pole and a second pole, wherein, when the drive magnet is stationary, the first pole and the second pole are angled relative to the longitudinal axis such that the first pole and the second pole are each positioned on both sides of the longitudinal axis. 11. A magnetic drive assembly for a haircutter, the magnetic drive assembly comprising: a drive shaft positioned along an axis of rotation; a bracket comprising: a body comprising: a first side wall; and a second side wall, the first side wall and the second side wall positioned on opposing sides of the drive shaft; a first magnet positioned on the first side wall; and a second magnet positioned on the second side wall; and a drive magnet coupled to the drive shaft, wherein the drive magnet generates a driving force to move the bracket. 12. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 11, the drive magnet further comprising a first pole and a second pole, wherein, when the drive magnet is in a first position, the first pole is 

positioned on a side of the axis of rotation and the second pole is positioned on an opposing side of the axis of rotation. 13. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 12, wherein, when the drive magnet is in the first position, the driving force further comprises an attractive force between the first pole and the first magnet and a repulsive force between the second pole and the second magnet such that the bracket moves in a first direction toward the attractive force. 14. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 13, wherein, when the drive shaft rotates the drive magnet into a second position, the first pole is positioned adjacent to the second magnet and the second pole is positioned adjacent the first magnet such that driving force comprises an attractive force between the first pole and the second magnet and a repulsive force between the second pole and the first magnet, and wherein the bracket moves in a second direction that is opposite the first direction. 15. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 1 1 , further comprising a retainer positioned behind the bracket, the retainer comprising: a first surface positioned distal to the first side wall of the bracket; a second surface positioned distal to the second side wall of the bracket; a first end magnet positioned on the first surface and facing toward the first side wall of the bracket; and a second end magnet positioned on the second surface and facing toward the second side wall of the bracket; wherein the first end magnet and the second end magnet generate a controlling force to limit movement of the bracket relative to the magnet. 16. A magnetic drive assembly for a haircutter, the magnetic drive assembly comprising: a drive shaft extending along an axis of rotation; a bracket comprising: a body comprising: a first side wall; and a second side wall, the first side wall and the second side wall positioned on opposing sides of the drive shaft; a first magnet positioned on the first side wall; and a second magnet positioned on the second side wall; a drive magnet coupled to the drive shaft, wherein the drive magnet generates a driving force to move the bracket; and a retainer , the retainer comprising: a first end magnet facing the first magnet; and a second end magnet facing the second magnet. 17. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 16, wherein the driving force generates linear movement of the bracket relative to the drive shaft. 18. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 17, wherein the first end magnet and the second end magnet generate a controlling force to provide resistance against the linear movement of the bracket. 19. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 16, wherein the drive magnet is a disc magnet that is diametrically polarized. 20. The magnetic drive assembly of claim 16, wherein the drive magnet is coaxial with the drive shaft.
